message:
fix(orbit): serialize saveToDisk with write lock + atomic rename (#8003) (#8007)

OrbitHandler.saveToDisk took a read lock (h.mu.RLock) before calling
saveToDiskLocked, which then ran os.WriteFile directly. Because
RLock allows multiple goroutines to enter concurrently, the
background scheduler (checkDueMissions) and any concurrent HTTP
request handler could both reach os.WriteFile at the same time and
interleave their writes — producing a truncated or garbled
orbit_missions.json that fails to Unmarshal on the next startup,
losing the user's scheduled missions.

Two fixes, belt and braces:

1. saveToDisk now takes h.mu.Lock() (write lock) so only one
   goroutine can serialize + write at a time. Updated the
   saveToDiskLocked doc comment to say the caller must hold the
   write lock, not "at least a read lock".

2. The write itself is now atomic: marshal to a temp file in the
   same directory, fsync, chmod, then rename(2) over the target.
   Rename is atomic on the same filesystem, so a concurrent reader
   (or a crash mid-write) always sees either the old complete file
   or the new complete file — never a partial one. Even if a
   future caller accidentally holds the wrong lock, an interrupted
   write cannot leave a corrupted target behind.

Credit to @Va16hav07 for the detailed reproduction and diagnosis.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
